senses.gloss: to flap the wings (of a bird)

etymology.text: A more primitive form of دَفَعَ (dafaʕa, “to push, to propel”), and its resultant noun, already formed before Arabic, though Aramaic דפא / ܕܦܐ (dappā, “board”) is believed to derive from Sumerian 𒁳 (DAB, “board”) / 𒁾 (DUB, “board”).

body.text: , (Lth, T, S, M, Mgh, Msb, K,) of anything, (Lth, T, M, Msb, K,) for instance, of a bird, (Msb,) and of a camel; (S;) as also ↓
More enduring than an old camel in whose sides are scabs formed over wounds:
a prov.]. (TA. [See Freytag's Arab. Prov. i. 737.]) And ↓
He passed the night turning over and over upon his sides
]. (TA.) The saying of' Antarah, describing his she-camel,

wakaA^an~amaA tanoA^aY bijaAnibi daf~ihaA A@lo
                      
                     
                        waHo$iY~i mino haziji AlEa$iY~i muw^aw~ami
Perseus transcribers were unclear about the final character of line 1 and the initial character of line 2.
And as though she were shrinking from the quarter of her off side,
being here used in the sense of
of ugly form and big head,
fearing to be scratched by it: as J says, [in art.
,] she shrinks with her off side because the rider's whip is in his right hand: (EM p. 233:) [or the meaning is,
as though she were shrinking with the outside of her off side;
with the side of her off side;
for, accord. to ISd,] this is an instance of the prefixing of a noun to another identical therewith [in signification]. (M.) [Hence also,]
May God smite him with the pleurisy
a part that faces one, above the foot
of sand; and of land or ground: (K:) accord. to En-Nadr, [the pl.]
parts that face one, above the foot
of land or ground; (T, TA;) as also
(TA.) ―         -b4-  See also the next paragraph. ―         -b5-  And
